mechanics.text:
COMBAT
INITIATIVE During turn; characters' phases are performed in cer- tain order; The process of determining the order which phas- es are executed called Initiative. At the beginning of each turn; every character rolls 2010 and adds his RFL Attribute to the result: The total is his Initiative score . Note that this roll is open ended Iike an Action Check; any 10s rolled are rolled again ad added to the Initiative total; Characters take their phases in Initiative order; starting with the character who has the highest Initiative score the event 0i the character with the higher RFL Attribute acts first. Further ties are resolved y an additional  tie-breaking die roll;
character can choose delay part of his phase atter using part 0i It. For example_ character can Simple Action t0 run into position and then declare that he will use nis second Simple Action make an attack against target of opportunity: the stated conditions do not come up at all in the turn, the character'$ delayed phase moves on into the following turn: does not roll Initiative but continues to delay from the previ- ous turn; phase can be held indefinitely in this way: At the end of any turn; character who is delaying can also choose to stop delaying and roll Initiative normally in the next turn_ When the delaying character takes his phase (or the part of his phase that was delayed), all his Action Checks for that phase have TN modifier of +2 because the character must react using snap judgment: Note that it is impossible for single character take more than one phase in single turn. Combat Sense: Characters with the Combat Sense Trait reduce the delaying modifier to +1_ Combat Paralysis: Characters with the Combat Paralysis Trait increase the delaying modifier to +3.
Initiative Modifiers The following skills, traits and effects modify character" Initiative roll or score as indicated Fatigue: character who has suffered   Fatigue see Fatigue_ P: 115) subtracts the number of Fatigue points he has suffered from the Initiative result. Combat Sense: Characters with the Combat Sense Trait roll 3010 and choose the best  two dice when   rolling Initiative Combat Paralysis: Characters with the Combat Paralysis Trait roll 3010 ad choose the worst two dice when rolling for Initiative_ Tactics: Characters with the Tactics Skill may add their skill bonus to their Initiative score provided the combat situation is appropriate t0 their Tactics knowledge For example character with only Tactics/ BattleMech would gain no bonus to Initiative in combat outside his Mech: Leadershlp: one character with the Leadership Skill is designated the leader of team of characters and he has some way t0 keep in touch with nis team (such as microcom municators). he can add his relevant Tactics Skill Bonus (if any) to the Initiative rolls of every character in his team he spends Simple Action that turn t0 lead the team However, the amount added may not exceed his skill bonus eadership For exam- leader with Leadership +1 and Tactics +3 could only add +1 t0 the Initiative totals of his teammates, although his per: sonal Initiative would Eain the full +3 Furthermore the Tactics Skill Bonuses of two or more characters are not additive, character being led would only gain the higher of his eader" his own Tactics Skill Bonuses_
Large Combats large combat   involving many characters may become unwieldy generate and keep track of Initiative for every character involved In these situations the gamemaster may employ one of the following options to speed up and sim: plify game play; The simplest way is for all the NPCs on each side to have single Initiative_ In tne most usual case this would mean all the enemy characters nave single Initiative _ while each player character generates his own Initiative_ there were any NPCs on the friendly side they would also generate single Initiative. Another   possibility each team roll single Initiative Note that this might not be all the characters on one side and all the characters on the other; it is likely in large  bat: Ile that the sides would be divided into tactical units such as squads this case_ each squad would generate Its Own Initiative_ either case only bonuses due t0 leaders who have Leadership and Tactics Skills apply; no other Initiative bonuses apply when generating 'mass" Initiative_ Add only the lowest RFL   from among all members of each side (or team) to its Initiative_
Delaying character need not take his phase when ne nas the opportunity but can instead delay his phase until later in the lurn The character must tell the gamemaster under what con- ditions he will take his action such as wait until see tar- get come around the corner; then want t0 shoot at it. The con ditions can be as vague specific as the player wants, though some condition ad reaction must be declared: i5 not accept able to simply state, My character will wait till later and then something;
MOVEMENT Movement in MW3 is kept fairly abstract for simplicity; no maps rulers  are needed most   combat situations; The range between characters should be tracked by the gamemas: ter for attack and movement purposes If the tactical situation complicated, map of the area can be sketched out paper; and the positions of each character can be marked pencil and updated as the battle progresses_ character s movement speed based on his Attributes and Running Skill ad depends whether he walking; runnirg
